title: Non-Expert Uses ChatGPT + AlphaFold to Design Custom mRNA Cancer Vaccine for Dog — Tumor Shrinks
summary: An Australian tech entrepreneur used ChatGPT, AlphaFold, and a custom-synthesized mRNA vaccine to treat his dog's terminal cancer. Researchers assisted and were reportedly excited; tumor size reduced significantly within weeks of the first injection. This is the clearest public demonstration yet of frontier AI enabling personalized medicine workflows outside of clinical settings — a genuine capability milestone, not hype.
angle: The real story isn't 'AI cured cancer' — it's that frontier AI tools (ChatGPT for research synthesis, AlphaFold for protein structure prediction) have lowered the barrier so dramatically that a non-expert could design a personalized mRNA vaccine. This is what democratized access to cutting-edge science actually looks like. The implications for personalized medicine, rare disease treatment, and veterinary care are massive.
